content:
🎖️ From Service to CEO: The 2026 Landscape

Veterans are 45 % more likely to start their own business than civilians.You have the leadership, the resilience, and the discipline. What you often lack is the initial capital.In 2026, the funding landscape for veteranpreneurs has expanded, with new "transition-focused" grants designed to turn your military skills into market dominance.

🚀 Top Private Grants for Veterans

While the government offers contracts, private foundations offer the cash.These are the top vetted programs for 2026.

⚔️

1. Warrior Rising

Grant + Mentorship

More than just a check, Warrior Rising is an ecosystem.Their process starts with "Warrior Academy" training. Top performers are then invited to pitch for grants to purchase equipment or inventory.

💡 Insider Tip:

Warrior Rising prioritizes "vetrepreneurs" who hire other veterans. If your business plan includes a hiring strategy for vets, highlight it bold and center.

🎗️

2. The Second Service Foundation

Pitch Competition($15k)

Formerly the StreetShares Foundation.They host the "Military Entrepreneur Challenge," a Shark Tank - style pitch competition. Events are held regionally and virtually throughout the year.

🚜

3. Farmer Veteran Coalition

Equipment Grants

For vets returning to the land.The "Fellowship Fund" buys pieces of equipment(tractors, greenhouses, hives) directly for veterans starting agricultural businesses.Awards range from $1,000 to $5,000.

🏛️ The "Hidden" Government Grant: VR & E

Veteran Readiness and Employment(Chapter 31)

Most vets know "Voc Rehab" for college tuition.But did you know there is a Self - Employment Track ? If your service - connected disability makes traditional employment difficult, the VA can purchase your startup equipment.

✅ What It Covers

  • • Computers, software, and office furniture.
  • • Tools, machinery, and vehicles(in some cases).
  • • Inventory to stock your shelves.
  • • Licensing and franchise fees.

❌ The Catch(Qualification)

  • • Must exist a service - connected disability rating(usually 20 % +).
  • • Must prove that your disability prevents "standard" employment.
  • • Requires a comprehensive business plan approved by a VRC counselor.

🦅 Federal Contracting: The 3 % Mandate

The federal government aims to award 3 % of all contracts to SDVOSBs (Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Businesses). This is a multi - billion dollar market.

Step 1: Obtain SDVOSB Status

Certification has moved from the VA to the SBA.You must apply through the VetCert portal.

Go to VetCert ↗

Step 2: No - Bid Contracts

Once certified, contracting officers can award "sole-source" contracts to you worth up to $5 million (manufacturing) without you having to bid against anyone else.

🎓 Training: Boots to Business

Before you borrow money, learn to manage it. Boots to Business(B2B) is the SBA's entrepreneurial education program offered on military installations worldwide. Completing it waives the application fees for SBA loans and opens doors to special lending rates.

❓ Frequently Asked Questions

Can I use my GI Bill to start a business ? ▼

Not directly for cash.You cannot withdraw GI Bill funds to buy inventory. However, you CAN use it to pay for non - degree business training programs, SBDC workshops, or entrepreneurship bootcamps that equip you to launch.

Are there grants for veteran spouses ? ▼

Yes! The Military Spouse Chamber of Commerce helps spouses get certified as business owners, and many private grants (like Second Service Foundation) have specific categories or openness to military spouses.

What is the Hivers and Strivers fund ? ▼

Hivers and Strivers is an Angel Investment Group , not a grant. They invest equity capital (usually $250k - $1M) into startups founded by graduates of U.S.Military Academies.
shortAnswer: Yes — 🇺🇸 Complete guide to grants and resources for veteran-owned small businesses (VOSB) and service-disabled veteran-owned small businesses (SDVOSB). Funding available: up to $15.
shortAnswerQuestion: What grants are available for veteran-owned businesses in 2026?

faq.question: Can I use my GI Bill to start a business?
faq.answer: Not directly for cash/inventory. You can use it for non-degree entrepreneurship training or workshops.

faq.question: Are there grants for veteran spouses?
faq.answer: Yes, many private foundations extend eligibility to military spouses, including the Second Service Foundation.

faq.question: How do I get SDVOSB certified?
faq.answer: You must apply through the **SBA VetCert** portal. Verification typically takes 30-60 days.

expertTip.title: Get Certified
expertTip.content: Obtaining your SDVOSB (Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business) certification is the single most important step. It unlocks sole-source federal contracts and specific set-aside grants.
